title: Cannot verify ADR-006 through ADR-009 have genuine alternatives — the single most common ADR defect (The Strawman ADR anti-pattern) cannot be checked without seeing the options tables.

Self-demonstration of §5.6 verification prompts is incomplete: individual element specs in PART II define 'DO NOT' constraints (demonstrating §3.8) but most lack explicit structured verification prompt blocks as required by INV-017 and §5.6 — the spec defines verification prompts without systematically applying them to its own element specs

§0.2.2 LLM consumption model is claimed as the foundational justification for all LLM provisions (referenced throughout) but does not appear in the document — without it, the causal chain for INV-011 through INV-015 and ADR-006 through ADR-009 is broken

The v1 file as provided is truncated — it begins mid-Chapter 9 (§9.2), meaning the most structurally important new content (INV-011–015, ADR-006–009, Gate 7, §0.2.2 LLM Consumption Model, §3.8 Negative Specifications element spec, §5.6 Verification Prompts, §5.7 Meta-Instructions, §1.4 end-to-end trace) cannot be independently verified — the Master TODO asserts these exist but the judge cannot evaluate their quality

The end-to-end trace (§1.4) is claimed in the Master TODO but not visible — this is the single most effective self-conformance check for a meta-spec, and its absence in the evaluable portion is a gap.
Multi-document specs (Open Question #2): cross-spec invariant referencing and ADR boundary conventions for systems with multiple DDIS specs remain unaddressed
Namespace note in §0.13 is stale (INV-001 through INV-019, ADR-001 through ADR-010) — an LLM author reading the modularization section will learn the wrong invariant count for the meta-standard

Cannot verify INV-011 through INV-015 are properly specified — each must have a plain-language statement, semi-formal expression, violation scenario, validation method, and WHY THIS MATTERS annotation per the format in §3.4. The Master TODO claims they are complete, but self-reported completion is not validation.

Gate 7 (LLM Implementation Readiness) is defined but not demonstrated — the gate requires an actual LLM to attempt implementation of a conforming spec and produce no hallucinations; without at least one worked LLM test case or falsification scenario, Gate 7 is aspirational rather than operational

ADR-009 ('LLM Provisions Woven Throughout') is asserted in the Master TODO as satisfied, but with the bulk of PART 0 and PART II missing from the provided file, it cannot be confirmed that provisions are genuinely woven vs. isolated in new sections — this is the highest-risk self-conformance claim

INV-015 (Implementation Ordering Explicitness) is claimed but not shown — ordering guarantees in specs are notoriously hard to falsify; the violation scenario and validation method need careful construction to avoid being an unfalsifiable invariant (error class per Appendix C)

ADR-006–009 text is absent: the problem / options / decision / WHY NOT / consequences structure for the four new ADRs is not shown, so INV-002 (decision completeness) and ADR authenticity cannot be checked

ADR supersession protocol remains an unresolved Open Question (§0.10.3 in v1): how a DDIS spec handles evolved or overturned ADRs has no prescribed procedure, leaving living specs with no formal evolution mechanism

INV-011–015 definitions are missing from the file: without seeing the formal expression, violation scenario, and validation method for each new invariant, INV-003 (falsifiability) cannot be verified for the additions that are the core contribution of v1

PART III and PART IV not verified for LLM-woven updates: ADR-008 mandates that every element specification include woven LLM guidance, but the voice guidance chapter (§8.1), anti-pattern catalog (§8.3), and operational playbook chapters were not reached in this review and may not yet carry the new DO NOT pattern

Appendix C (Specification Error Taxonomy) and Appendix D (Quick-Reference Card) are declared in the document structure but their content was not verified — high risk they are placeholder stubs rather than fully authored appendices, which would violate Gate 1 Structural Conformance
Machine-readable cross-references (Open Question #1): no defined syntax for automated reference graph construction; cross-reference validation remains a manual O(sections²) audit

The §5.6 self-bootstrapping verification prompt (the explicit checklist for a DDIS-conforming spec) has only 6 checks and does not include an INV-020 check — the standard's own demonstration of verification prompts is not updated to cover its newest invariant

Critical: The next iteration must produce the COMPLETE document, not a tail segment. The most important claimed improvements (LLM consumption model §0.2.2, INV-011–015 with violation scenarios, ADR-006–009 with genuine alternatives, Gate 7 specification, §3.8/§5.6/§5.7 element specs) cannot be evaluated because they are in the missing ~60% of v1.

§3.8 (negative specifications element spec) and §5.6 (verification prompts element spec) are claimed complete in Part X but are absent from the file; these are the most important new element specifications and cannot be evaluated
Re-evaluate on next iteration
